title: Neoachmandra pentaphylla
taxonomy: Neoachmandra pentaphylla is a species of plant in the family Achariaceae. It belongs to the genus Neoachmandra, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Neoachmandra pentaphylla is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Rosids, Order - Malpighiales, Family - Achariaceae, Genus - Neoachmandra, Species - N. pentaphylla. The exact classification of this plant may be subject to change as new research emerges. The classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.
uses: Neoachmandra pentaphylla has been traditionally used in medicine in various cultures, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are beneficial in treating wounds and infections. Its extracts have also been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In addition, Neoachmandra pentaphylla is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections.
